reasoning:
The base rate for 'Explosions/Remote violence' in the Maldives is historically near zero. There have only been a handful of such events in the last twenty years (e.g., 2007, 2015, and 2021). Thus, the background probability of an explosive or remote violence event occurring in the country in any given 30-day window is around 1–2%. For the question to resolve to YES, there needs to be > 0.000 average in the 30-day window preceding each resolution date, which equals at least 1 event. I conducted extensive web searches for news of explosions, drone strikes, landmines, or terrorist attacks in the Maldives from January 1, 2026, to today (February 15, 2026). No events were found. 

For the first resolution date (Feb 22, 2026), 23 days of the 30-day window have already passed with no events, leaving only a 7-day period for an event to occur. The probability of an event in 7 days is fractionally small (well under 1%), so my estimate sits at the minimum allowed bound (0.02). 

For the second resolution date (Mar 17, 2026), the 30-day window lies entirely in the future (Feb 15 to Mar 17). The chance of an unforeseen event happening within a full month is slightly higher than for 7 days, but still matches the very low historical base rate of ~1-2%. To respect the guideline that uncertainty must increase with the forecast horizon, I am predicting 0.03 for the second date.
